source_metadata.title_raw: Noah Starr - I-18-001 - February 1, 2018 - San Francisco

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 1, 2018

Noah Starr
10 Crestline Drive, Apt. #10
San Francisco, CA 94131

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-18-001

Dear Mr. Starr;

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.² Because your request seeks general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.³

QUESTION

May you accept a position as an independent contractor with California Consulting, LLC, while simultaneously employed by the State Board of Equalization?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit a state employee from accepting simultaneous employment with a private company. However, should you accept private employment, the Act prohibits you from taking part in decisions with a foreseeable and material effect on your financial interests, such as your employer or consulting business. Additionally, your agency may impose restrictions on incompatible activities outside of the Act which may restrict your ability to accept this position. You should consult your agency's counsel regarding your agency's restrictions on incompatible activities.

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² We note Section 1090 prohibits an official from making or participating in the making of a contract in which the official has a financial interest. Generally, as a state employee, you may not make or participate in the making a contract involving your private employer.

³ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-18-001
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are a Deputy District Director with the CA State Board of Equalization, Office of
Board Member Fiona Ma. You are considering simultaneous private employment as an independent
contractor with California Consulting, LLC.

Your duties with the state include outreach to stakeholder groups to educate and inform
constituents on tax and fee programs, representing Board Member Ma at community events,
working with nonprofit and governmental organizations to produce public workshops and seminars
on compliance with state tax regulations, and managing an intern program and District Office
operations. Planned duties with California Consulting include attending meetings with elected
officials, city managers, superintendents; attending city council meetings and school board
meetings; preparing presentations for potential clients utilizing company marketing materials; and
conducting business development and new client recruitment.

ANALYSIS

There is nothing in the Act that precludes you from employment with a private company
while also employed by the state. However, the Act's conflict of interest provisions do prohibit you
from making, participating in making or otherwise using your official position to influence a
governmental decisions in which you have a financial interest. (Section 87100.) An official has a
financial interest in a decision only if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a
member of the official's immediate family, or on any of the official's interests as specified in
Section 87103.

Accordingly, if you accept employment with California Consulting and a decision before
you as a state employee implicates the company, a client of the company, or your consultant
activities, you may have a conflict of interest that will be disqualify you from taking part in the
decision. If you need assistance determining whether you are disqualified from any particular
decision, you should seek additional advice describing the nature of the governmental decision.⁴

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Assistant General Counsel

BGL:jgl

⁴ Note that you are also prohibited under the Act from taking part in a decision as a state employee if the
decision will have a foreseeable and material effect on a prospective employer with which you are negotiating or have
an arrangement for future employment. (Section 87407; Regulation 18747.)

sections.question:
May you accept a position as an independent contractor with California Consulting, LLC, 
while simultaneously employed by the State Board of Equalization?
sections.conclusion:
The Act does not prohibit a state employee from accepting simultaneous employment with a 
private company. However, should you accept private employment, the Act prohibits you from 
taking part in decisions with a foreseeable and material effect on your financial interests, such as 
your employer or consulting business. Additionally, your agency may impose restrictions on 
incompatible activities outside of the Act which may restrict your ability to accept this position. 
You should consult your agency's counsel regarding your agency's restrictions on incompatible 
activities.2 We note Section I 090 prohibits an official from making or participating in the making of a contract in which 
the official has a financial interest. Generally, as a state employee, you may not make or participate in making a 
contract involving your private employer.

written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329( c )(3 ). )
